steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank sits between the noun "profile" and the prepositional phrase "in the Navajo Times on August 22, 2013." The blank must supply a modifier that attaches directly to the noun without requiring a relative pronoun. The grammar rule: a participial (-ing) form can directly modify a preceding noun; finite verb forms (appears, appeared, has appeared) cannot — they require "that" to form a relative clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — appearing: A present participle directly modifies the noun — "a profile appearing in the Navajo Times" is a well-formed noun phrase. ✓
  • Choice B — has appeared: A finite present-perfect verb cannot modify a noun without "that" — "a profile has appeared in the Navajo Times" turns the main clause into a sentence fragment or creates a run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — appeared: A finite past-tense verb cannot directly modify a noun without "that" — "a profile appeared in the Navajo Times" would be a separate clause disconnected from "was the subject of." ✗
  • Choice D — appears: A finite present-tense verb has the same problem as C — it cannot modify the noun directly. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
